title: Job Openings at Talent Pool Resources International Limited
content:
Talent Pool Resources International Limited - No.1 Human Resources Business Partnerfor talent acquisition, job placements, business networking and general services. We offer innovative HR Solutions to Small and medium scale business accross Nigeria.

We are recruiting to    fill the following positions below:

1.) Inventory / Asset Control Officer (Spare Parts)

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are urgently seeking a detail-oriented and reliable officer to manage spare parts inventory and company assets within our transport and logistics operations.
	The role ensures availability of critical vehicle parts, proper storage, and accurate record-keeping to support efficient fleet operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and store vehicle spare parts properly
	Maintain a clean, organized, and secure store
	Issue spare parts for maintenance with proper approval and documentation
	Monitor stock levels and ensure critical parts are always available
	Track fast-moving and slow-moving items
	Conduct regular stock counts and resolve discrepancies
	Maintain records of fleet assets (vehicles, tools, equipment)
	Track movement and usage of parts and assets
	Prepare all inventory and usage reports
	Ensure compliance with company procedures and support audits.


Requirements


	OND / HND / BSc in a relevant field
	4 - 6 years of experience in inventory/storekeeping roles
	Industry experience in transport, logistics, fleet management, or automotive spare parts is required
	Good knowledge of vehicle spare parts and inventory processes
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory systems
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
	Honest, reliable, and able to work independently.


Salary
Competitive.

Application Closing Date: 6th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

Note: Preferrably applicants who lives in Lagos can apply as there is NO Accomodation.

 

2.) Ultrasound Technician

Job Location: Ayobo, Lagos
Employment Type: Part-time
Specialty: Pelvic & Full Abdominal Scans
Schedule: Twice weekly

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a qualified Ultrasound Technician to perform pelvic and full abdominal ultrasound scans.
	The role involves producing accurate diagnostic images to support clinical decision-making.


Responsibilities


	Perform pelvic and full abdominal ultrasound scans
	Prepare and position patients correctly for scans
	Operate ultrasound equipment safely and effectively
	Ensure clear, high-quality diagnostic images
	Maintain proper patient records and documentation
	Follow clinical and safety guidelines


Requirements


	2–3 years of experience as an Ultrasound Technician or sonographer
	Certification or qualification in Medical Imaging/Radiography
	Experience in pelvic and abdominal scanning is a MUST
	Good communication skills
	Attention to detail and professionalism.


Rate
?1,500/hour (9am – 4pm).

Application Closing Date: 27th April, 2026. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Ultrasound scan and their location" as subject of the mail.

Note: There is no accommodation, preferred candidates should live within Iyana Ipaja, Dopemu, Ayobo, Egbeda and its environs.

 

3.) Facility Engineer (Mid-Level)

Job Location: Alimosho, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Retail

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Facility Engineer to manage and maintain our retail facilities in Alimosho.
	The ideal candidate will ensure all building systems, including HVAC, electrical, and plumbing, operate efficiently, safely, and in compliance with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily facility operations, including HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning), electrical, and plumbing systems
	Monitor and maintain HVAC systems to ensure optimal performance and indoor air quality
	Conduct routine inspections and preventive maintenance of equipment and infrastructure
	Diagnose and resolve faults in HVAC and other building systems
	Respond promptly to maintenance issues and emergency repairs
	Manage vendors and contractors for facility-related services, including HVAC servicing
	Ensure compliance with safety regulations and company policies
	Monitor utility usage and implement cost-saving initiatives, especially energy efficiency for HVAC systems
	Maintain accurate maintenance records and reports
	Support store operations by ensuring minimal downtime of facilities


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, or related field)
	3–4 years’ experience in facility management, with strong HVAC experience (retail experience is an advantage)
	Solid knowledge of HVAC systems, electrical systems, and plumbing
	Experience in troubleshooting and maintaining HVAC equipment
	Good problem-solving and organizational skills
	Ability to manage multiple tasks and work independently
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and maintenance management systems is an added advantage


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail
	Technical expertise (especially HVAC systems)
	Time management
	Communication and interpersonal skills
	Health & safety awareness


Compensation & Benefits


	?250,000 gross monthly salary
	13th month salary (end-of-year bonus)
	Pension contributions in line with statutory requirements
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO) coverage
	Paid annual leave
	Opportunities for professional development.


Application Closing Date: 20th April, 2026. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using Job Position and Location as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head, Bancassurance",
  "description": "Ecobank Transnational Incorporated (ETI), a public limited liability company, was established as a bank holding company in 1985 under a private sector initiative spearheaded by the Federation of West African Chambers of Commerce and Industry with the support of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Head, Bancassurance

Job Identification: 1307
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description
Strategy execution &amp; business growth:


	Lead the execution of the Nigeria Bancassurance strategy in full alignment with Group priorities, ensuring consistent translation of strategic objectives into actionable country-level initiatives.&nbsp;
	Drive insurance penetration across all customer segments by integrating insurance propositions into core banking products, customer journeys, and lifecycle engagement points.
	Scale standalone insurance sales across all distribution channels by activating frontline teams and embedding insurance into both physical and digital sales processes.&nbsp;
	Deliver sustainable revenue growth in line with agreed targets by optimizing product mix, improving conversion rates, and strengthening renewal income streams.&nbsp;
	Drive product innovation and localization by adapting Group solutions to the Nigerian market context and identifying new opportunities aligned to customer needs and regulatory requirements.&nbsp;


Distribution &amp; sales enablement:


	Embed Bancassurance as a core performance metric across all frontline channels, ensuring insurance is actively sold / referred alongside core banking products and services.&nbsp;
	Build and sustain a high-performance sales culture by aligning incentives, reinforcing accountability, and driving consistent execution across branches, RMs, DSAs, and digital channels.&nbsp;
	Strengthen capability building programs through structured training, certification, and ongoing coaching to enhance product knowledge and sales effectiveness.&nbsp;
	Support frontline teams through joint client engagements, provision of sales tools, and access to insurance specialists to improve conversion and customer outcomes.&nbsp;
	Drive digital enablement by integrating insurance into digital channels and platforms, enabling seamless customer journeys, self-service capabilities, and improved scalability.&nbsp;


Partnerships &amp; commercial management:


	Manage strategic relationships with insurance partners, ensuring alignment to business objectives, service standards, and long-term growth ambitions.&nbsp;
	Negotiate and continuously optimize commercial terms to ensure competitiveness, profitability, and alignment with market benchmarks and internal expectations.&nbsp;
	Drive partner accountability through clearly defined SLAs, regular performance reviews, and enforcement of agreed service delivery standards.&nbsp;
	Identify and onboard new partners where necessary and in line with the Group&rsquo;s Bancassurance Framework to strengthen product offerings, enhance competitiveness, and address gaps in capability or market coverage.&nbsp;


Customer value proposition &amp; product management:


	Develop and continuously refine compelling customer value propositions that address the needs of different segments and enhance the overall customer offering.&nbsp;
	Ensure all products are relevant, competitive, and fully compliant with regulatory requirements, internal policies, and customer protection standards.&nbsp;
	Drive product rationalization by simplifying the product suite, focusing on high-impact offerings, and eliminating duplication or underperforming products.&nbsp;
	Monitor and continuously improve customer experience across the insurance lifecycle, including onboarding, servicing, claims, and renewals.&nbsp;


Performance management &amp; business intelligence:


	Track and monitor key performance metrics across products, channels, and segments to ensure delivery against business targets and strategic objectives.&nbsp;
	Implement robust reporting dashboards and management information systems to provide timely, accurate, and actionable insights.&nbsp;
	Drive data-led decision-making by leveraging analytics to identify trends, optimize performance, and inform strategic interventions.&nbsp;
	Provide comprehensive monthly performance reporting to country and Group stakeholders, highlighting progress, risks, and corrective actions.&nbsp;
	Ensure revenue assurance by validating commission flows, minimizing leakage, and maintaining transparency in financial performance.&nbsp;


Risk, governance &amp; compliance:


	Ensure full compliance with all local regulatory requirements, Group policies across all activities.&nbsp;
	Adherence to the Group Bancassurance Framework, including governance, product approval, sales conduct, and partner management standards.&nbsp;
	Proactively manage conduct risk by ensuring fair customer treatment, appropriate sales practices, and transparent disclosures across all channels.&nbsp;
	Maintain strong internal controls and audit readiness by ensuring proper documentation, monitoring, and timely resolution of identified gaps.&nbsp;


Stakeholder management &amp; leadership:


	Act as the country lead for Bancassurance, providing clear direction, leadership, and accountability for business performance and execution.&nbsp;
	Engage effectively with key stakeholders across the bank, including business heads, risk, compliance, finance, and operations, to ensure alignment and support.&nbsp;
	Collaborate closely with Group Bancassurance and other central teams to drive consistency, leverage best practices, and deliver on strategic priorities.&nbsp;
	Lead, develop, and motivate the Bancassurance team, building a strong talent pipeline and fostering a high-performance and inclusive culture.&nbsp;


Requirements
Education &amp; experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree required.&nbsp;
	12&ndash;15 years of experience.&nbsp;
	Professional Insurance qualifications a must-have.&nbsp;
	Strong knowledge of the Nigerian Insurance industry.&nbsp;


Key competencies:


	Strategic thinking&nbsp;
	Commercial acumen&nbsp;
	Bancassurance expertise&nbsp;
	Data-driven mindset&nbsp;
	Leadership capability&nbsp;
	Governance discipline&nbsp;


Success measures:


	Revenue growth&nbsp;
	Increased penetration&nbsp;
	Channel productivity&nbsp;
	Customer experience improvement&nbsp;
	Partner performance&nbsp;
	Compliance adherence

Head, Bancassurance Job at Ecobank Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Ecobank Transnational Incorporated (ETI), a public limited liability company, was established as a bank holding company in 1985 under a private sector initiative spearheaded by the Federation of West African Chambers of Commerce and Industry with the support of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Bancassurance

Job Identification: 1307
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Job Type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description
Strategy execution & business growth:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Lead the execution of the Nigeria Bancassurance strategy in full alignment with Group priorities, ensuring consistent translation of strategic objectives into actionable country-level initiatives. 
	Drive insurance penetration across all customer segments by integrating insurance propositions into core banking products, customer journeys, and lifecycle engagement points.
	Scale standalone insurance sales across all distribution channels by activating frontline teams and embedding insurance into both physical and digital sales processes. 
	Deliver sustainable revenue growth in line with agreed targets by optimizing product mix, improving conversion rates, and strengthening renewal income streams. 
	Drive product innovation and localization by adapting Group solutions to the Nigerian market context and identifying new opportunities aligned to customer needs and regulatory requirements. 


Distribution & sales enablement:


	Embed Bancassurance as a core performance metric across all frontline channels, ensuring insurance is actively sold / referred alongside core banking products and services. 
	Build and sustain a high-performance sales culture by aligning incentives, reinforcing accountability, and driving consistent execution across branches, RMs, DSAs, and digital channels. 
	Strengthen capability building programs through structured training, certification, and ongoing coaching to enhance product knowledge and sales effectiveness. 
	Support frontline teams through joint client engagements, provision of sales tools, and access to insurance specialists to improve conversion and customer outcomes. 
	Drive digital enablement by integrating insurance into digital channels and platforms, enabling seamless customer journeys, self-service capabilities, and improved scalability. 


Partnerships & commercial management:


	Manage strategic relationships with insurance partners, ensuring alignment to business objectives, service standards, and long-term growth ambitions. 
	Negotiate and continuously optimize commercial terms to ensure competitiveness, profitability, and alignment with market benchmarks and internal expectations. 
	Drive partner accountability through clearly defined SLAs, regular performance reviews, and enforcement of agreed service delivery standards. 
	Identify and onboard new partners where necessary and in line with the Group’s Bancassurance Framework to strengthen product offerings, enhance competitiveness, and address gaps in capability or market coverage. 


Customer value proposition & product management:


	Develop and continuously refine compelling customer value propositions that address the needs of different segments and enhance the overall customer offering. 
	Ensure all products are relevant, competitive, and fully compliant with regulatory requirements, internal policies, and customer protection standards. 
	Drive product rationalization by simplifying the product suite, focusing on high-impact offerings, and eliminating duplication or underperforming products. 
	Monitor and continuously improve customer experience across the insurance lifecycle, including onboarding, servicing, claims, and renewals. 


Performance management & business intelligence:


	Track and monitor key performance metrics across products, channels, and segments to ensure delivery against business targets and strategic objectives. 
	Implement robust reporting dashboards and management information systems to provide timely, accurate, and actionable insights. 
	Drive data-led decision-making by leveraging analytics to identify trends, optimize performance, and inform strategic interventions. 
	Provide comprehensive monthly performance reporting to country and Group stakeholders, highlighting progress, risks, and corrective actions. 
	Ensure revenue assurance by validating commission flows, minimizing leakage, and maintaining transparency in financial performance. 


Risk, governance & compliance:


	Ensure full compliance with all local regulatory requirements, Group policies across all activities. 
	Adherence to the Group Bancassurance Framework, including governance, product approval, sales conduct, and partner management standards. 
	Proactively manage conduct risk by ensuring fair customer treatment, appropriate sales practices, and transparent disclosures across all channels. 
	Maintain strong internal controls and audit readiness by ensuring proper documentation, monitoring, and timely resolution of identified gaps. 


Stakeholder management & leadership:


	Act as the country lead for Bancassurance, providing clear direction, leadership, and accountability for business performance and execution. 
	Engage effectively with key stakeholders across the bank, including business heads, risk, compliance, finance, and operations, to ensure alignment and support. 
	Collaborate closely with Group Bancassurance and other central teams to drive consistency, leverage best practices, and deliver on strategic priorities. 
	Lead, develop, and motivate the Bancassurance team, building a strong talent pipeline and fostering a high-performance and inclusive culture. 


Requirements
Education & experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree required. 
	12–15 years of experience. 
	Professional Insurance qualifications a must-have. 
	Strong knowledge of the Nigerian Insurance industry. 


Key competencies:


	Strategic thinking 
	Commercial acumen 
	Bancassurance expertise 
	Data-driven mindset 
	Leadership capability 
	Governance discipline 


Success measures:


	Revenue growth 
	Increased penetration 
	Channel productivity 
	Customer experience improvement 
	Partner performance 
	Compliance adherence
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at ABNL Limited
content:
ABNL Limited was incorporated in 1992, as an Oil and Gas servicing company, with a vision to provide professional engineering and technical services to the Oil and Gas exploration and production companies in Nigeria and the West African sub-region. Since its incorporation the company has grown in leaps and bound, expanding its business horizon but still within the Oil and Gas sector.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below: 

1.) Construction Superintendent III

Job Location: Nigeria
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for planning, coordinating, and controlling the day-to-day field operations for the assigned crafts and subcontractors.
	A typical scope of responsibility would be Field Superintendent or Area Superintendent.


Responsibilities


	In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks, a Level 3 Construction Superintendent will:
	Assist in preparation and review for submittals, RFIs, and RFCs, and make recommendations regarding suitability  
	Review contractor health and safety plans, traffic control, and erosion control measures on projects
	Verify quantities for payment
	Identify non-conformance issues with construction work and help develop potential resolutions of non-conformance issues
	May serve as the lead Company representative at sub-sites where the Construction Site Manager or Construction Site Lead typically does not reside
	Provide construction core competency expertise when assigned to the function
	Ensure good communications and relations with the contractors


Requirements


	Experience in field construction planning, execution, and completions
	Willing to travel (domestic/overseas) to project sites
	Past Construction Superintendent experience on a major capital project
	Familiar with Construction Materials Testing procedures and ASTM standards
	Enthusiastic, self-starter with a desire to provide excellent service to clients.


Application Closing Date: 22nd April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV in MS Word format to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Project Engineer II

Job Location: Seplat House, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Main Functions


	Performs engineering design evaluations and works to complete projects within budget and schedule constraints.
	Reviews industry policies and procedures to ensure data accuracy, security, and regulatory compliance.
	Reviews reports on production, malfunctions, and maintenance to identify and resolve issues.
	Provides technical support for the engineering process.
	Relies on instructions and pre-established guidelines to perform job functions.
	Works under immediate supervision.
	Primary job functions do not typically require exercising independent judgment.
	Provides backup support to the Project Manager as required.


Tasks and Responsibilities


	Participates in Invitation to Tender (ITT) development, bid evaluation, and contractor selection
	Participates in the identification and sourcing of long-lead equipment, ensuring Front End Engineering Design (FEED) contractor support until equipment is assigned to the contractor
	Coordinates the development of CCAP (Company Capital Project Management) Gate 3 deliverables
	Coordinates the development of the Functional Interaction Plan
	Coordinates the project issue resolution process
	Coordinates change requests and endorses change authorizations
	Responsible for the completion of assigned work scope in line with project objectives
	Engages the Project Team and functional resources to ensure design and construction align with project specifications and standards
	Serves as the interface between the Project Team and contractor
	Ensures smooth transitions between project stages
	Leads the Project Management Team (PMT) in applying Project Management Systems


Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in an Engineering field
	Experience across multiple assignments in engineering, construction, and project management and controls activities
	Broad range of experience and skills across multiple functions and project assignments (multi-discipline skill set)
	Strong knowledge of design standards, specifications, and work processes
	Strong leadership, influencing, and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational, coordination, planning, and interface management skills
	Ability to work under tight deadlines, heavy workloads, and changing priorities
	Excellent communication skills with proficiency in reading, writing, and speaking English
	Proficient in Microsoft Office suite of software programs


Application Closing Date: 22nd April, 2026. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV in MSword format to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Projects - Construction Coordinator III

Job Location: Ohaji, Imo
Employment Type: Contract
Work Schedule: Standard - 5 days on / 2 days off - 5 days on / 2 days off

Job Description


	The Construction Coordinator is responsible for construction planning, coordinating construction input, and communicating with Construction Management. Establishes the Construction Planning Program (CPP) and seeks alignment of the planning scope with the Construction Advisor and subsequently with the Construction Support Manager.
	Manages assigned projects and the implementation contractors involved to ensure that project deliverables are completed for RFI, meet quality requirements and schedules, and are delivered in line with project requirements.


Tasks and Responsibilities
In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks, a Level 3 Construction Coordinator will:


	Be able to identify issues, problems, and opportunities; make timely decisions; develop appropriate and innovative solutions; and seek assistance when required.
	Be able to work on multiple projects of varying sizes and complexity.
	Be able to work effectively in small to large multidisciplinary teams, and share and transfer knowledge within teams and stakeholders.


Skills and Qualifications


	B.Sc / M.Sc Degree in Engineering
	Good coordination and interface capabilities
	Previous experience in a closely related position and/or project environment
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to convey information and ideas to individuals and groups, and make presentations when required
	A person of integrity who is trusted by others and consistently honors commitments


Application Closing Date: 17th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume in MSword format to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Procurements Officer (Construction) Job at CADSYNC Consults Limited

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurements Officer (Construction)",
  "description": "CADSync Consults is a forward-thinking management consulting firm that delivers personalized learning solutions and consulting services aimed at triggering behavior modification and attitudinal change. We specialize in helping individuals and organizations unlock their potential through strategic insights, behavior-focused programs, and impactful interventions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurements Officer (Construction)

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Procurement Officer is responsible for sourcing, negotiating, purchasing, and managing goods and services required by the organization in a cost-effective, timely, and compliant manner.
	The role ensures value for money, vendor reliability, and adherence to internal policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities
Procurement &amp; Sourcing:


	Identify, evaluate, and select reliable suppliers and vendors in line with organizational needs.
	Obtain and analyze quotations, bids, and proposals to ensure competitive pricing and quality.
	Prepare purchase requisitions, purchase orders, and contract documentation.
	Ensure timely procurement of goods and services to avoid operational delays.


Vendor &amp; Contract Management:


	Negotiate pricing, payment terms, delivery timelines, and service levels with suppliers.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with vendors and service providers.
	Monitor supplier performance and address issues relating to quality, cost, or delivery.
	Ensure contracts are executed and managed in line with agreed terms


Compliance &amp; Policy Adherence:


	Ensure all procurement activities comply with organizational policies, ethical standards, and applicable regulations.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, documentation, and audit trails.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required procurement documentation.


Cost Control &amp; Reporting:


	Track procurement spend and support cost-saving initiatives.
	Prepare procurement reports, supplier evaluations, and spend analyses.
	Assist in budgeting and forecasting related to procurement activities.


Inventory &amp; Coordination


	Work closely with finance, operations, and stores/warehouse teams to align procurement with demand.
	Monitor stock levels and support inventory replenishment planning.
	Follow up on deliveries to ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Experience with procurements in the construction space
	Professional certifications (e.g., CIPS, CPSM) are an added advantage.


Key Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong negotiation and vendor management skills.
	Excellent analytical and cost-evaluation abilities.
	Good knowledge of procurement processes and contract management.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and procurement/ERP systems.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested &amp; qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@cadsyncconsults.com&nbsp;using Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Procurements Officer (Construction) Job at CADSYNC Consults Limited




        
    

        
        CADSync Consults is a forward-thinking management consulting firm that delivers personalized learning solutions and consulting services aimed at triggering behavior modification and attitudinal change. We specialize in helping individuals and organizations unlock their potential through strategic insights, behavior-focused programs, and impactful interventions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurements Officer (Construction)

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Procurement Officer is responsible for sourcing, negotiating, purchasing, and managing goods and services required by the organization in a cost-effective, timely, and compliant manner.
	The role ensures value for money, vendor reliability, and adherence to internal policies and regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities
Procurement & Sourcing:


	Identify, evaluate, and select reliable suppliers and vendors in line with organizational needs.
	Obtain and analyze quotations, bids, and proposals to ensure competitive pricing and quality.
	Prepare purchase requisitions, purchase orders, and contract documentation.
	Ensure timely procurement of goods and services to avoid operational delays.


Vendor & Contract Management:


	Negotiate pricing, payment terms, delivery timelines, and service levels with suppliers.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with vendors and service providers.
	Monitor supplier performance and address issues relating to quality, cost, or delivery.
	Ensure contracts are executed and managed in line with agreed terms


Compliance & Policy Adherence:


	Ensure all procurement activities comply with organizational policies, ethical standards, and applicable regulations.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, documentation, and audit trails.
	Support internal and external audits by providing required procurement documentation.


Cost Control & Reporting:


	Track procurement spend and support cost-saving initiatives.
	Prepare procurement reports, supplier evaluations, and spend analyses.
	Assist in budgeting and forecasting related to procurement activities.


Inventory & Coordination


	Work closely with finance, operations, and stores/warehouse teams to align procurement with demand.
	Monitor stock levels and support inventory replenishment planning.
	Follow up on deliveries to ensure accuracy, completeness, and timeliness.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Experience with procurements in the construction space
	Professional certifications (e.g., CIPS, CPSM) are an added advantage.


Key Skills & Competencies:


	Strong negotiation and vendor management skills.
	Excellent analytical and cost-evaluation abilities.
	Good knowledge of procurement processes and contract management.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and procurement/ERP systems.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested & qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
